name: Curcumin
mechanism: NF-kB inhibition; Nrf2 activation; antioxidant

name: Luteolin
mechanism: Inhibits PKC, NF-kB, mast cell + microglia activation; crosses BBB

name: Rosmarinic acid
mechanism: Anti-allergic; mast cell stabilization

name: Thymoquinone
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; histamine pathway inhibition

name: Baicalein
mechanism: Potent mast cell stabilizer; 12-LOX inhibitor

name: EGCG
mechanism: Mast cell stabilization; antioxidant; Nrf2 activator

name: Montelukast
mechanism: CysLT1 receptor antagonist; blocks LTD4/LTE4

name: Eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA)
mechanism: SPM precursor; reduces LTB4; mast cell modulation

name: Docosahexaenoic acid (DHA)
mechanism: SPM precursor; anti-inflammatory; membrane modulation

name: Cholecalciferol (Vitamin D3)
mechanism: Immunomodulation; mast cell stability; T-reg support

name: Palmitoylethanolamide
mechanism: PPAR-alpha agonist + indirect endocannabinoid 'entourage' (raises AEA tone) + direct mast-cell stabilization (ALIA pathway, Levi-Montalcini coinage)

name: Piceatannol
mechanism: **Direct SYK kinase inhibitor** (the proximal FcεRI signaling kinase in mast cells); also Nrf2 activator; resveratrol's hydroxylated metabolite

name: Resveratrol
mechanism: Sirtuin / Nrf2 activator; MRGPRX2 inhibition

name: Myricetin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; xanthine oxidase inhibition; ROS scavenger; weak Nrf2 induction

name: Fisetin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; senolytic (clears senescent cells); Nrf2 induction; SYK + STAT signaling suppression

name: Testosterone
mechanism: Androgen receptor agonist; **mast cells express AR**; testosterone has documented MAST-CELL-STABILIZING effect (lowers degranulation in male-vs-female studies); sex-hormone modulation of MCAS is real but under-studied

name: Apigenin
mechanism: Mast cell stabilizer; NF-kB inhibition

name: Butyric acid
mechanism: HDAC inhibition; gut barrier reinforcement; Treg induction

name: Alpha-lipoic acid
mechanism: Recycles GSH/Vit C/E; Nrf2 activator

name: Rutin
mechanism: Antioxidant; histamine release inhibition

name: Kaempferol
mechanism: Antioxidant; anti-inflammatory; mast cell modulation

name: 6-Gingerol
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; prostaglandin inhibition; mast cell stability

name: Naltrexone (low-dose)
mechanism: TLR4 antagonism; glial / immune modulation at low dose

name: Delta-9-THC
subcategory: Phytocannabinoid (intoxicating; controlled substance, US Schedule I as of pre-2024 status)
mechanism: CB1/CB2 partial agonist; mast-cell-stabilizing in vitro via CB2

name: Cannabidiol
mechanism: CB2 weak agonist + TRPV1/TRPA1 modulator + 5-HT1A partial agonist; mast-cell stabilizer in vitro; PPAR-gamma; some Nrf2 induction

name: Silibinin
mechanism: Hepatoprotective; antioxidant; mast cell modulation

name: Hesperidin
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; capillary stabilization

name: Calcitriol
mechanism: VDR agonist; downregulates mast cell IgE response

name: Xanthohumol
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; NF-κB + Nrf2 modulation; phyto-estrogen (mild)

name: Myricitrin
mechanism: Antioxidant; anti-allergic

name: Cannabidiolic acid
mechanism: **Selective COX-2 inhibitor** (more potent than CBD); 5-HT1A agonist; non-intoxicating

name: Linalool
mechanism: Anxiolytic + anti-inflammatory + mast-cell stabilizer in vitro

name: Beta-caryophyllene
mechanism: Selective CB2 agonist (Ki ~155 nM) — unique among dietary terpenes; mast-cell stabilizer; anti-inflammatory in colitis/arthritis models

name: Alpha-bisabolol
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; mast-cell stabilizer in vitro; gastric-mucosa protective

name: Galangin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; STAT6 suppression; PGE2 inhibition

name: Naringenin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; CYP3A4 inhibitor (grapefruit interaction); anti-inflammatory; Nrf2 induction

name: D-Limonene
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; GSH induction; mast-cell stabilization (LAD2 in vitro)

name: N-Acetylcysteine (NAC)
mechanism: GSH replenishment; ROS scavenging; Nrf2 activation (indirect)

name: Chrysin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer in vitro; Nrf2 inducer; weak aromatase inhibitor

name: Hesperetin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; anti-allergic in OVA-sensitized mice; weak Nrf2

name: Ellagic acid
mechanism: Nrf2 activator; gut-microbiome-converted to urolithins; anti-inflammatory

name: Eucalyptol
mechanism: Mucolytic; anti-inflammatory (NF-kB suppression); bronchodilator

name: Nicotinamide riboside
mechanism: Crosses cell membrane via nucleoside transporters; converted intracellularly to NAD+; SIRT-pathway support

name: Magnolol
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; PPAR-γ partial agonist; GABA-A modulator

name: Wogonin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer (LAD2 + RBL-2H3); Akt/MAPK suppression; Nrf2 activation; sister flavone of Baicalein

name: Honokiol
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer (RBL-2H3 + mouse BMMC); SIRT3 activator; CB2 partial agonist

name: Biotin
mechanism: Cofactor for carboxylases; downregulates degranulation (animal data)

name: Tangeretin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; NF-κB suppression; better BBB penetration than parent flavonoids

name: Nobiletin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer + neuroprotective + CB1 indirect; potent anti-inflammatory

name: Ivermectin
subcategory: Macrocyclic lactone antiparasitic — off-label mast-cell stabilizer evidence
mechanism: Glutamate-gated chloride channel agonist (parasitic primary); secondary mast-cell stabilization via P2X4 antagonism (Yan 2019); GABA-A allosteric (CNS at high dose); P-glycoprotein substrate

name: Delta-8-THC
subcategory: Phytocannabinoid (intoxicating but less potent than delta-9)
mechanism: CB1/CB2 agonist; biased toward CB2 vs delta-9

name: Pterostilbene
mechanism: Resveratrol analog with better bioavailability; SIRT1 activator; Nrf2 induction; mast-cell stabilizer

name: Taurine
mechanism: Membrane stabilization; osmoregulation; anti-inflammatory

name: Methylene blue
mechanism: Alternative mitochondrial electron carrier (bypasses Complex I/III); MAO-A inhibitor; cGMP/sGC modulator; weak anti-inflammatory + anti-oxidative-stress at low dose; mast-cell evidence preclinical

name: Oleoylethanolamide
mechanism: PPAR-alpha agonist; anti-inflammatory + satiety signal

name: Cannabichromene
mechanism: TRPA1 agonist; endocannabinoid-system modulator; anti-inflammatory

name: Tetrahydrocannabinolic acid
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; non-intoxicating (does not cross BBB); decarboxylates to THC with heat

name: Cannabichromenic acid
subcategory: Phytocannabinoid acid (raw CBC precursor — PubChem CID may resolve to CBC)
mechanism: Anti-microbial; precursor to CBC

name: Tetrahydrocannabivarin
mechanism: CB1 neutral antagonist + CB2 partial agonist (dose-dependent)

name: Cannabidivarin
mechanism: TRPA1/TRPV1 modulator; CB1/CB2 weak affinity; anti-inflammatory

name: Geraniol
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; mast-cell stabilizer in vitro

name: Beta-pinene
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; anti-microbial; bronchodilator

name: Cannabigerol
mechanism: CB1/CB2 weak partial agonist; alpha-2 adrenergic agonist; 5-HT1A antagonist; anti-inflammatory

name: Anandamide
mechanism: CB1/CB2 partial agonist + TRPV1; endogenous endocannabinoid

name: Nerolidol
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; transdermal penetration enhancer; anti-microbial

name: Phloretin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; GLUT1/2 inhibitor; topical applications

name: NAD+
mechanism: Universal redox cofactor + substrate for sirtuins (SIRT1-7), PARPs, CD38; mast-cell ADP-ribosylation + SIRT-mediated NF-κB suppression; mitochondrial energy

name: Beta-myrcene
mechanism: Sedative; analgesic; anti-inflammatory; weak mu-opioid potentiator

name: Acacetin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer in vitro; NF-κB + COX-2 suppression

name: Cannabinol
mechanism: CB2-biased partial agonist; sedative

name: Alpha-pinene
mechanism: Bronchodilator; acetylcholinesterase inhibitor (mild); anti-inflammatory

name: 2-Arachidonoylglycerol
mechanism: CB1/CB2 full agonist; major endocannabinoid signal

name: Terpinolene
mechanism: Antioxidant; sedative; mild anti-inflammatory

name: Isorhamnetin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; downstream metabolite of quercetin; PPAR-γ + Nrf2

name: Nicotinamide mononucleotide
mechanism: Direct intermediate in NAD+ salvage pathway; raises tissue NAD+ better than NAD+ itself orally

name: Alpha-humulene
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory (TNF-alpha / IL-1beta suppression); analgesic

name: Cannabicyclol
mechanism: Minor cannabinoid; very limited pharmacology

name: Cannabigerolic acid
mechanism: PPAR-α agonist; precursor to CBGA→CBG/THCA/CBDA; SARS-CoV-2 spike binding (preclinical)

name: Methyl red
subcategory: Azo dye / pH indicator (NOT a clinical therapeutic)
mechanism: Azobenzene with carboxylic acid; weak antimicrobial in vitro; NO clinical therapeutic use

name: Lycopodine
mechanism: Weak acetylcholinesterase inhibitor; weak NMDA antagonist; very limited mast-cell data; mostly anti-tumor preclinical

name: Dupilumab
mechanism: Fully human IgG4 mAb binding IL-4 receptor alpha → blocks IL-4 AND IL-13 signaling → suppresses Th2 cascade (IgE production, eosinophil recruitment, mast-cell maturation/priming, mucus, barrier disruption). NOT a direct mast-cell blocker — works upstream by quieting the Th2 environment that primes mast cells.

name: Bovista (Lycoperdon)
subcategory: Puffball mushroom extract (Lycoperdon bovista / L. perlatum)
mechanism: Mixture — primary constituents include calvacin (anti-tumor polysaccharide), various sterols, possibly hemolytic principles. Homeopathically used for urticaria + chemical sensitivity.
